query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Relocation', 'The act of moving or moving something or someone from one place to another', 'Over time, the community learned to relocation its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reluctant', 'Unwilling or hesitant; showing resistance', 'Her reluctant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remand', 'To send back', 'Over time, the community learned to remand its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remediable', 'Capable of being fixed', 'The reviewer described the tone as remediable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remedial', 'Intended to correct or improve something', '“He enrolled in remedial classes to improve his reading skills.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remembrance', 'A retained memory', 'Her remembrance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reminisce', 'To recall the past in a private moment', 'Critics argued that the plan might reminisce the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remiss', 'Lacking care or attention to duty', 'Over time, the community learned to remiss its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remit', 'To forgive, pardon', 'The author hoped the new policy would remit tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remonstrance', 'A protest', 'Scholars noted a striking remonstrance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remorse', 'A feeling of regret or sadness for doing wrong or sinning', 'Her remorse became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Remunerate', 'To compensate, to pay', 'Critics argued that the plan might remunerate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renaissance', 'A rebirth or revival', 'The essay highlights the renaissance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renascence', 'A new beginning or rebirth, regeneration', 'The essay highlights the renascence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rend', 'To separate into parts with force or sudden violence', 'Critics argued that the plan might rend the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Render', 'To cause to become', 'Critics argued that the plan might render the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rendezvous', 'To meet at an agreed time and place', 'Over time, the community learned to rendezvous its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rendition', 'An interpretation, as of a role or a piece of music', 'Her rendition became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renegade', 'An outlaw or rebel', 'The essay highlights the renegade of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renege', 'To break a promise or commitment, to go back on one''s word', 'The author hoped the new policy would renege tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renounce', 'To give up, resign, surrender', 'Over time, the community learned to renounce its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renovate', 'To restore to good condition', 'The author hoped the new policy would renovate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renown', 'Fame, celebrity, wide recognition', 'Her renown became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Renunciation', 'The act of rejecting or renouncing something as invalid', 'The essay highlights the renunciation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reorganize', 'To organize something again, or in a different manner', 'Critics argued that the plan might reorganize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reparable', 'Able to be fixed', 'The author hoped the new policy would reparable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reparation', 'The making of amends for wrong or injury done', 'The essay highlights the reparation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repartee', 'A quick, witty reply', 'Her repartee became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repast', 'A meal', 'Scholars noted a striking repast between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repeal', 'To cancel, invalidate, annul', 'Over time, the community learned to repeal its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repel', 'To drive or force back', 'Critics argued that the plan might repel the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repentance', 'Deep sorrow for a past error', 'The essay highlights the repentance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repentant', 'Penitent', 'Her repentant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repercussion', 'A consequence or ensuing result of some action', 'Scholars noted a striking repercussion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repertoire', 'A collection of skills or works that a person can perform', '“The pianist’s repertoire included both classical and modern pieces.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repertory', 'A presentation in which a company presents several works', 'Scholars noted a striking repertory between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repetition', 'The act or an instance of doing something again', 'The essay highlights the repetition of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repine', 'To be fretful, discontented', 'Over time, the community learned to repine its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Replace', 'To take the place of something or someone; to substitute', 'The author hoped the new policy would replace tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Replenish', 'To refill, to renew', 'Over time, the community learned to replenish its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Replete', 'Full of', 'The passage presents a replete portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Replica', 'An exact copy', 'Her replica became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Replicate', 'Reproduce results under same conditions', 'Critics argued that the plan might replicate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repose', 'To lay, to set down', 'The author hoped the new policy would repose tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repository', 'A location for storage, often for safety or preservation', 'The essay highlights the repository of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reprehend', 'To criticize, to reprove', 'Over time, the community learned to reprehend its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reprehensible', 'Deserving censure or condemnation', 'The passage presents a reprehensible portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Repress', 'To keep under control, or suppress', 'Critics argued that the plan might repress the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reprieve', 'A temporary relief from something unpleasant', '“The judge granted a reprieve, delaying the execution.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reprimand', 'To reprove in a formal or official way', 'The author hoped the new policy would reprimand tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
